tools.name: capital_change_brief
tools.description: Compose the current evidence-backed capital-change brief for one ticker. Use when a user asks what changed in reported institutional ownership, which tracked managers drove it, whether qualifying Form 4 open-market activity points in the same or opposite direction, and wants the SEC proof. Current-o

tools.name: fund.list
tools.description: List the institutional managers (hedge funds, asset managers) that file SEC 13F reports, ranked by AUM. Returns each fund's name, AUM, latest reported quarter, holdings count, and its SEC CIK — the id you pass to fund.holdings, fund.holdings.diff, fund.holdings.as_of, and fund.position.history. Star

tools.name: copy.leaderboard
tools.description: Rank the institutional managers (13F funds) worth copying. Each row is a followable actor — name, manager, book size (AUM), holdings count, latest reported quarter, follower count, the slug (for copy.target.get), and CIK (to follow via watchlist / pull via fund.holdings). Price-derived copy-return f

tools.name: copy.target.get
tools.description: Get one copy target (13F fund) by slug (from copy.leaderboard): its latest disclosed book — the top holdings you'd mirror, each with % of book and its quarter-over-quarter change (NEW/ADDED/REDUCED/EXITED) — plus book size, latest quarter, and filing history. Pair with watchlist tools to follow it.

tools.name: fund.holdings
tools.description: Latest reported 13F equity holdings for a fund (by SEC CIK) — top positions by value, each with its CUSIP and quarter-over-quarter change (new/added/reduced/exited). Public-domain SEC EDGAR data.

tools.name: search
tools.description: Resolve a name to a tracked entity. Turn a fund or manager name (e.g. 'Berkshire', 'Citadel') into the SEC CIK or slug you pass to the fund.* tools — the entity-resolution entry point. Searches funds and managers by name or identifier.

tools.name: congress.disclosures.list
tools.description: List the US House financial-disclosure filing INDEX for a year (STOCK Act). filingType 'P' = Periodic Transaction Report (stock trades). Returns one row per filing with a link to the source PDF — this is the index, NOT parsed trades. Parsed line-items (member, ticker, buy/sell, amount range) are ava

tools.name: congress.member.search
tools.description: Find US House representatives who filed financial disclosures in a year, by name (filing index — parsed trades available via congress.trades, US House live; Senate being added).

tools.name: congress.trades
tools.description: Parsed STOCK Act transaction line-items: actual congressional stock trades by ticker or by member — buy/sell, the disclosed amount range, trade + notification dates, and owner (self/spouse). Each row links to its source PTR PDF. US House (e-filed) is live; Senate is being added. Pass `ticker` OR `me

tools.name: clinicaltrials.search
tools.description: Upcoming clinical-trial readout catalysts. For an industry-sponsored interventional Phase 2/3 drug trial, an ESTIMATED primaryCompletionDate is the expected topline-readout date — the binary event that re-rates a biotech ticker. Filter by sponsor (lead sponsor, INDUSTRY class), condition, phase, and

tools.name: fda.approvals
tools.description: FDA drug-approval / rejection actions (Drugs@FDA, CDER). For a sponsor or drug, returns the regulatory action history: action=approved (AP, bullish), complete_response_letter (CR, a rejection — sharply bearish), or tentative_approval (TA). Flags new molecular entities (NME) and priority review — the

tools.name: fda.recalls
tools.description: FDA drug recalls (Recall Enterprise System). Class I = could cause death/serious harm (material adverse event); Class II/III lower. Filter by recalling firm, drug, classification, and recency; returns classification, reason, status, dates, and the recalling firm's resolved ticker (null if private).

tools.name: pharma.resolve
tools.description: Resolve a pharma sponsor / manufacturer name (e.g. 'Janssen Pharms', 'AstraZeneca AB') to its public ticker, so an FDA approval / trial readout / recall can be joined to 13F ownership, insider (Form 4) buys, and the confluence signal. Returns ticker, company name, exchange, and resoluution method (al

tools.name: macro.series.get
tools.description: Get recent observations for a macro/economic time series from FRED (e.g. GDP, CPIAUCSL, DGS10, UNRATE).

tools.name: macro.series.search
tools.description: Search FRED for macro/economic time series by keyword; returns matching series IDs and titles.

tools.name: predictionmarket.list
tools.description: List prediction-market contracts with implied probabilities, from Polymarket and/or Kalshi. Optional keyword filter.

tools.name: predictionmarket.quote
tools.description: Get a single prediction-market contract's implied probability by platform + id (Polymarket slug or Kalshi ticker).

tools.name: divergence.scan
tools.description: Cross-market divergence: for a topic keyword, pull matching contracts from Polymarket and Kalshi and compare implied probabilities to surface potential mispricing/arb. Note: matches are keyword-correlated, not verified same-event — confirm the contracts describe the same outcome before trading.

tools.name: fund.holdings.as_of
tools.description: Point-in-time holdings: a fund's reported positions AS KNOWN ON a given date (no look-ahead — the most recent filing whose filedAt <= as_of). Built for backtesting.

tools.name: fund.position.history
tools.description: Time-series of a single security position (by ticker or CUSIP) across a fund's filings — shares and USD value per quarter.

tools.name: fund.holdings.diff
tools.description: Quarter-over-quarter change set for a fund's most recent filing: new positions, adds, reduces, exits, and reviewed reporting-universe changes.

tools.name: events.latest
tools.description: Live SEC filing-event stream: SEC Form 4 (insider) filings today, with 8-K and 13D/G on the roadmap. Poll with `since` (a monotonic cursor) to get every filing newer than your last call; the response's next_cursor advances it. Filter by form or tickers; a form we don't carry yet returns an explicit

tools.name: watchlist.poll
tools.description: Poll THIS workspace's watchlist for new filings since a cursor: every SEC Form 4 (insider) filing on the tickers your team follows, newest-after-cursor (8-K + 13D/G are on the roadmap). Pass `since` (a prior next_cursor) to get only what's new; built for a watch loop. Curate the watchlist in the Ark

tools.name: watchlist.poll.live
tools.description: Like watchlist.poll but LIVE freshness — includes filings accepted within the last 24h (minute-fresh, normalized within minutes of SEC acceptance). Premium-priced and requires the Developer or Pro plan; other plans poll delayed (≤24h-old) freshness via watchlist.poll. Same workspace-watchlist scope

tools.name: insider.transactions
tools.description: Insider (SEC Form 4) transactions — open-market buys/sells, awards, option exercises — for a company (by ticker) or a person (by insider name). Pass `signal: true` to restrict to open-market buys/sells (codes P/S), the tradeable subset. Pass `since` to diff only new filings.

tools.name: insider.filing
tools.description: One SEC Form 4 filing by accession number: the reporting insider, issuer, and every transaction in it (non-derivative + derivative), with net open-market flow and a link to the original SEC document. The full-filing follow-up when insider.transactions or events.latest hands you an accession_number.

tools.name: insider.company
tools.description: Net insider activity for one company over a window: open-market buy vs sell value/shares and distinct buyer/seller counts. Answers 'are insiders net buying or selling this name right now?'

tools.name: insider.clusters
tools.description: Market-wide insider cluster scan: companies where several distinct insiders made open-market buys (or sells) inside a window — the high-conviction cluster signal. Returns tickers ranked by distinct-insider count and total value.

tools.name: stock.owners
tools.description: Institutional (13F) owners of a stock by ticker — which funds hold it, position value, share count, and the quarter-over-quarter change. The 'who owns this stock' join across all tracked funds.

tools.name: signal.crowding
tools.description: Cross-fund 'smart money' consensus from every tracked fund's latest 13F: the securities the MOST funds are collectively holding (mode='held'), buying this quarter (mode='bought' — NEW/ADDED cluster-buys), or selling (mode='sold' — REDUCED). The inverse of stock.owners: surfaces WHAT to look at, rank

tools.name: signal.confluence
tools.description: The moat join — where do informed INSIDERS (Form 4 open-market buys), institutional SMART MONEY (13F cluster-buys), and CONGRESS (US House STOCK Act trades) AGREE? Pass a `ticker` for all three legs on one name (insider + fund + congress net buy/sell + a verdict that escalates to triple_confluence_b

tools.name: capital_rotation
tools.description: THE flagship 'where is smart money rotating' signal. Aggregates quarter-over-quarter SEC 13F position changes across ALL tracked institutions into per-SECTOR capital flows: which sectors institutions are net BUYING into vs SELLING out of this quarter, with breadth (how many funds agree), net flow as

tools.name: get_smart_money_leaderboard
tools.description: Which disclosed 13F books are most focused? Ranks eligible institutional funds by a PRICE-FREE score: breadth-adjusted top-10 focus (40%), reported book growth (20%), and evidence-adjusted insider co-buy (40%). A 10-name book has neutral focus evidence, exact ties share a rank input, and sparse co-b

tools.name: get_ticker_confluence
tools.description: For ONE ticker: do the independent disclosure sources AGREE? Returns the cross-source confluence verdict — institutions (13F) × insiders (Form 4) × Congress (STOCK Act) all buying/selling the same name — PLUS the capital-rotation context: which sector the ticker is in and whether smart money is rota

tools.name: provenance.get
tools.description: Trust layer: return the source record(s) for a datapoint — origin URL, fetch time, parser version — so the agent can cite primary sources.

tools.name: account.balance
tools.description: FREE (0 credits). Return this API key's credit balance (free + paid), USD value, and top-up URL. Call this to self-monitor before/after spending — checking your balance never costs credits.

tools.name: feedback.submit
tools.description: FREE (0 credits). Tell the Arkolith team what data you wish existed, report wrong/missing data, or suggest an improvement. For capital_change_brief, call this only after the user explicitly says the delivered answer was useful or says what was missing; include workflow, brief_id, ticker, and verdict

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: clinicaltrials.search, watchlist.poll, provenance.get · Private data: fund.list, copy.leaderboard, clinicaltrials.search, fda.approvals, fda.recalls, pharma.resolve, insider.filing, get_smart_money_leaderboard, provenance.get, account.balance, feedback.submit · Public sink: stock.owners, get_smart_money_leaderboard, feedback.submit.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: clinicaltrials.search, watchlist.poll, provenance.get · Private data: fund.list, copy.leaderboard, clinicaltrials.search, fda.approvals, fda.recalls, pharma.resolve, insider.filing, get_smart_money_leaderboard, provenance.get, account.balance, feedback.submit · Public sink: stock.owners, get_smart_money_leaderboard, feedback.submit

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "fund.holdings.diff" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "(hash 721d5ab0)" → after: "Quarter-over-quarter change set for a fund's most recent filing: new positions, adds, reduces, exits, and reviewed reporting-universe changes."

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "get_smart_money_leaderboard" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "(hash 5fd8aa9d)" → after: "Which disclosed 13F books are most focused? Ranks eligible institutional funds by a PRICE-FREE score: breadth-adjusted top-10 focus (40%), reported book growth (20%), and evidence-adjusted insider co-buy (40%). A 10-name book has neutral focus evidence, exact ties share a rank input, and sparse co-b"

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.
